sentence: She came up with a brilliant solution to the problem
explanation: <Come up with> means to think of or produce an idea.

sentence: The company had to lay off hundreds of workers last year
hint: Subject + had to + phrasal verb + object + time
explanation: <Lay off> means to dismiss employees, usually for economic reasons.

sentence: He managed to get away with cheating on the test
hint: Subject + managed to + phrasal verb + gerund
explanation: <Get away with> means to do something wrong without being punished.

sentence: We need to crack down on illegal parking in the city centre
hint: Subject + need to + phrasal verb + object + place
explanation: <Crack down on> means to take strict action against something.

sentence: She has been putting up with his bad behaviour for too long
hint: Subject + present perfect continuous + phrasal verb + object
explanation: <Put up with> means to tolerate something unpleasant.

sentence: The detective was trying to piece together the evidence from the crime scene
hint: Subject + was trying to + phrasal verb + object + place
explanation: <Piece together> means to assemble information gradually.

sentence: They decided to do away with the old regulations
hint: Subject + decided to + phrasal verb + object
explanation: <Do away with> means to abolish or eliminate.

sentence: He was looking forward to starting his new job next month
hint: Subject + was + phrasal verb + to + gerund + object
explanation: <Look forward to> is followed by a gerund (-ing form).

sentence: The government plans to phase out fossil fuel subsidies by 2030
hint: Subject + plans to + phrasal verb + object + time
explanation: <Phase out> means to gradually stop using or providing something.

sentence: She couldn't keep up with the pace of the advanced class
explanation: <Keep up with> means to maintain the same speed or level as others.

sentence: The manager asked us to cut back on unnecessary expenses
hint: Subject + asked + object + to + phrasal verb + object
explanation: <Cut back on> means to reduce the amount of something.

sentence: He finally owned up to making the mistake in the report
hint: Subject + adverb + phrasal verb + to + gerund
explanation: <Own up to> means to admit responsibility for something.
